questions.q: In which Indian state does the Gomateshwara statue stand?
questions.explanation: The Gomateshwara statue is located at Shravanabelagola in the state of Karnataka.

questions.q: The Gomateshwara statue depicts which revered figure?
questions.explanation: The colossal statue represents the Jain saint Bahubali, also known as Gomateshwara.

questions.q: With which Indian religion is Gomateshwara most closely associated?
questions.explanation: Bahubali is a deeply venerated figure in Jainism, and the statue is a major Jain pilgrimage icon.

questions.q: At which town is the Gomateshwara statue located?
questions.explanation: The statue stands atop Vindhyagiri hill at Shravanabelagola in Hassan district, Karnataka.

questions.q: Approximately how tall is the Gomateshwara monolith?
questions.explanation: The monolithic statue stands roughly 17 metres (about 57 feet) tall.

questions.q: What is special about how the Gomateshwara statue was made?
options: It is assembled from many granite blocks
It is carved from a single block of stone
It is built of brick and plaster
questions.explanation: Gomateshwara is a monolithic statue, carved out of a single block of granite rock.

questions.q: In what posture is the Gomateshwara statue carved?
questions.explanation: Bahubali is shown standing erect in the kayotsarga meditative posture of bodily abandonment.

questions.q: Around which century CE was the Gomateshwara statue carved?
questions.explanation: The statue was carved around 981 CE, placing it in the 10th century.

questions.q: What is the grand head-anointing ceremony of the statue called?
questions.explanation: The statue is ceremonially bathed and anointed in the famous Mahamastakabhisheka festival.

questions.q: On top of what natural feature does the statue stand at Shravanabelagola?
questions.explanation: The statue is installed on the summit of Vindhyagiri (also called Indragiri) hill.

questions.q: Who is generally credited with commissioning the Gomateshwara statue?
questions.explanation: The statue was commissioned by Chamundaraya, a minister and commander of the Western Ganga dynasty.

questions.q: Under which dynasty's patronage was the Gomateshwara statue erected?
questions.explanation: Chamundaraya served the Western Ganga dynasty, which ruled parts of Karnataka at the time.

questions.q: According to Jain tradition, Bahubali was the son of which Tirthankara?
questions.explanation: Bahubali was a son of Rishabhanatha (Adinatha), the first Jain Tirthankara.

questions.q: What is depicted climbing the legs and arms of the Gomateshwara statue?
questions.explanation: Creepers, vines and serpents are carved around his limbs, symbolizing his years of unmoving meditation.

questions.q: Why is Bahubali shown with creepers entwining his body?
options: To show he was a warrior
To show he was a king
To show he stood motionless in meditation for a very long time
To show he was a sculptor
questions.explanation: Tradition holds he meditated standing so long that vines grew up his body, signifying his renunciation.

questions.q: How often is the great Mahamastakabhisheka ceremony traditionally held?
questions.explanation: The Mahamastakabhisheka is celebrated roughly once every twelve years at Shravanabelagola.

questions.q: The name 'Shravanabelagola' partly refers to which feature of the town?
questions.explanation: The name relates to the white pond (bela kola) between the two hills of the sacred town.

questions.q: Shravanabelagola is located in which district of Karnataka?
questions.explanation: Shravanabelagola lies in Hassan district of Karnataka.

questions.q: Which Jain sect's tradition does the nude depiction of Bahubali reflect?
questions.explanation: The fully nude form reflects the Digambara ('sky-clad') Jain tradition of total renunciation.

questions.q: During the Mahamastakabhisheka, the statue is bathed with substances including which of these?
options: Milk, saffron, sandalwood paste and other offerings
questions.explanation: Devotees pour milk, water, saffron, sandalwood paste, turmeric and other sacred substances over the statue.

questions.q: Visitors must do what in order to reach the Gomateshwara statue at the summit?
options: Take a boat across a lake
Climb a long flight of rock-cut steps
questions.explanation: Pilgrims traditionally climb hundreds of steps cut into the Vindhyagiri hill to reach the statue.

questions.q: What is the literal meaning often given for the title 'Gomateshwara'?
questions.explanation: Bahubali came to be venerated as Gommata, and 'Gomateshwara' honours him as the revered Lord Gommata.

questions.q: From which primary type of rock is the Gomateshwara monolith carved?
questions.explanation: The statue was hewn from the fine-grained granite gneiss of the Vindhyagiri hilltop.

questions.q: According to Jain legend, against whom did Bahubali fight before renouncing the world?
questions.explanation: Bahubali contested kingship with his brother Bharata but renounced victory and worldly life to pursue liberation.

questions.q: Many ancient inscriptions at Shravanabelagola are written chiefly in which language?
questions.explanation: The hills are dotted with numerous old inscriptions, predominantly in Kannada, recording grants and devotees.

questions.q: The two sacred hills at Shravanabelagola are Vindhyagiri and which other?
questions.explanation: The smaller hill is Chandragiri, associated with the sage Bhadrabahu and emperor Chandragupta Maurya in Jain tradition.

questions.q: Which Mauryan emperor is traditionally said to have spent his last days as a Jain ascetic at Shravanabelagola?
questions.explanation: Jain tradition holds that Chandragupta Maurya renounced his throne and lived as an ascetic on Chandragiri hill here.

questions.q: Around which year CE is the consecration of the Gomateshwara statue traditionally dated?
questions.explanation: The statue is traditionally dated to around 981 CE, during the late Western Ganga period.

questions.q: The standing meditative posture in which Bahubali is carved is technically called what?
questions.explanation: Kayotsarga is the upright, motionless 'dismissing of the body' posture seen in the Bahubali statue.

questions.q: Inspired by Shravanabelagola, later large Bahubali monoliths in Karnataka were also erected at places such as which town?
questions.explanation: Later monolithic Bahubali statues followed at Karnataka sites like Karkala and Dharmasthala, echoing the Shravanabelagola model.
